items.title: Iran war derailed talks to claw back billions in tax credits paid to miners

children.content: Billions in tax credits are paid to coal miners every year. Ministers discussed clawing back some of that money in the May federal budget, but the Iran war has pushed out those talks.

gallery.altText: A female mine worker walks past a mine dump truck at a coal mine in central Queensland.

thumbnail.altText: A female mine worker walks past a mine dump truck at a coal mine in central Queensland.

items.title: The BHP files reveal a big problem facing the mining giant

children.content: For a company that prides itself on digging out the metals for the world's clean energy future, saying nothing about its own clean energy future is not an option.

image.altText: Mining infrastructure and machinery in pits of red dirt.

items.title: Woman in nine-year battle with insurer as failed roof repairs cause mould

children.content: Lily lodged an insurance claim with her provider GIO Suncorp after a truck ran through her street's power lines. Nearly a decade later, she has been forced out of her home.

image.altText: Lily looks at the camera in her garden wearing a red shirt

items.title: Women and children linked to ISIS spend first night back in Australia

children.content: A group of so-called ISIS brides who left the notorious al-Roj camp in Syria last week have spent their first night home in Australia.

image.altText: A woman in back seat of a car wearing a face mask.

items.title: Despite growing up online, students' digital literacy is declining

children.content: Australian students have recorded their worst-ever results in national tests that measure digital literacy, with just 37 per cent of year 10 students and 50 per cent of year 6 students assessed as proficient.

image.altText: A pair of unidentifiable hands type on a laptop, with other people on laptops in the background.

items.title: Report claims AI boom could keep Australia using coal and gas for longer

children.content: A new report concludes the AI-fuelled surge of power-hungry data centres across Australia is jeopardising the country's energy transition.

gallery.altText: a man walks between rows of servers

thumbnail.altText: a man walks between rows of servers

items.title: Scott's sacking gives Essendon another chance to step out of Hird shadow

children.content: Essendon finds itself at a clear demarcation point after sacking Brad Scott as the shadow of James Hird once again looms large over the troubled club.

image.altText: Brad Scott pictured during his Essendon coaching tenure

items.title: Complaint lodged with Canada's privacy commissioner over Australian court breach

children.content: Litigants in at least 146 court matters were potentially involved in a data breach that is now the subject of a formal complaint with the privacy commissioner in Canada, Senate estimates has been told.

image.altText: A man in a navy suit and glasses and maroon tie speaking into microphone.

items.title: Europe bakes in unprecedented heatwave as UK records tumble

children.content: The British capital's previous hottest May day was set only one day prior, when the mercury topped 33.5C.

gallery.altText: A man drinking from a water bottle out the front of Big Ben.
Hundreds of people dotted across a wide beach with a pier in the background, on a sunny day.

items.title: 'Very scary and sad': Travellers in limbo after company enters liquidation

children.content: An Australian travel company selling heavily discounted international holidays has collapsed, leaving customers scrambling and their plans in disarray.

image.altText: A middle aged woman leaning on a motorbike, smiling

items.title: Navy chief says Chinese flotilla in Tasman Sea had 'impact'

children.content: Australia's Chief of Navy says China stirred "anger" in New Zealand when it sent a powerful naval flotilla into the Tasman Sea last year, encouraging political support on both sides of the ditch for increased defence spending.

image.altText: Mark Hammond sitting in a chair on stage speaking to Stephen Dziedzic.

items.title: State of Origin is tonight. Here is what you need to know for Game I

children.content: The fiercest rival in Australian rugby league resumes tonight, as New South Wales and Queensland meet in the opening match of the 2026 men's State of Origin series.

gallery.altText: Cameron Munster of the Maroons is tackled by Nathan Cleary of the Blues

thumbnail.altText: Cameron Munster of the Maroons is tackled by Nathan Cleary of the Blues

items.title: Wallabies facing same selection headache as State of Origin's NSW Blues

children.content: Just as NSW Blues coach Laurie Daley had a difficult selection decision to make, Joe Schmidt may be thinking long and hard about one position in the Wallabies' back line for July's Nations Championship Tests.

image.altText: Max Jorgensen runs onto the field for the Waratahs.

items.title: CBA boss says reconsider applying CGT change to non-passive assets

children.content: The Commonwealth Bank boss speaks out about the government's tax changes.

image.altText: Commonwealth Bank chief executive Matt Comyn looks at the camera in an office setting.

items.title: What to do when you get unexpected ancestry DNA test results

children.content: Direct-to-consumer genetic testing kits are often given as gifts or purchased "as a bit of fun", which means people often aren't prepared to discover unexpected parents, siblings and other relatives.

gallery.altText: man looking at phone with shocked expression

thumbnail.altText: man looking at phone with shocked expression

avatar.alt: Kellie Scott black and white headshot

items.title: Blak Douglas is an Archibald winner. He says the art prize needs to change

children.content: The acclaimed Dunghutti artist is using his latest exhibition to challenge perceptions of Aboriginal art and the systemic issues creatives face in Australia.

image.altText: Artist Blak Douglas, born Adam Douglas Hill, has opened a new exhibition at NSW Parliament House entitled 'Home Flown'.

items.title: Quiz: Who has scored more tries in State of Origin games in Sydney?

children.content: If you think you know your stuff when it comes to State of Origin, here's your chance to prove it.

items.title: US military strikes Iranian boats and missile launch sites

children.content: US Centcom says the strikes were designed "to protect our troops from threats posed by Iranian forces".

articles.title: Why Trump's push to expand his Abraham Accords was met with silence

children.content: As US President Donald Trump pushes for a deal with Iran, he is reviving one of the signature foreign policy projects of his first term: the Abraham Accords.

image.altText: A photo of Benjamin Netanyahu, Donald Trump and Abdullah bin Zayed sitting at a long table, holding up copies of signed deals

articles.title: Trump calls on Arab nations to 'immediately sign the Abraham Accords'

children.content: Donald Trump has boldly tied negotiations to end his war with Iran to a swathe of Arab nations formally establishing diplomatic ties with Israel.

image.altText: Israeli PM Netanyahu shakes hands with US President Donald Trump, who points at him.

gallery.altText: two us military fighter jets take off over the ocean

thumbnail.altText: two us military fighter jets take off over the ocean

items.title: Bondi gunman remained on counter-terror radar in 2022

children.content: Naveed Akram and his father travelled to Uzbekistan around 2022 and a former terror hotspot in the Philippines shortly before committing the Bondi massacre.

image.altText: Extereme close-up of a man with dark hair and a moustache

items.title: Four dead, including two children, after train hits Belgian school bus

children.content: The train collided with a minibus, which was carrying nine people at the time, at a level crossing.

image.altText: Rescue workers stand near a wrecked bus in Belgium.

items.title: Archibald Prize sitter calls out APY art collective mistreatment

children.content: Award-winning artist Iluwanti Ken says the withdrawal of funding and support over the "white hands on black art" scandal has left her feeling like a stranger in her own industry.

gallery.altText: A seated Aboriginal woman holds a microphone and gestures with her free hand.
Iluwanti Ken, in her late 70s, stands in front of an intricately detailed black-and-white painting of eagles hung in a gallery.

thumbnail.altText: A seated Aboriginal woman holds a microphone and gestures with her free hand.
Iluwanti Ken, in her late 70s, stands in front of an intricately detailed black-and-white painting of eagles hung in a gallery.

avatar.alt: Hannah Story, a blonde woman sitting outside smiles. A dog is at her feet, and she wears a T-shirt that reads "MIRANDA JULY".

items.title: Iron ore processing project dumped by BHP for not being profitable enough

children.content: Internal BHP documents reveal how the mining giant planned to set up an iron ore processing facility in the Pilbara to cut carbon emissions before backing away from it entirely.

image.altText: A photo of an iron ore mine is ripped down the middle and replaced by a photo of a BHP building.

items.title: One woman will make Japanese history by going on maternity leave

children.content: A Japanese mayor will take maternity leave this year, becoming the first in the country's history to do so while in office.

image.altText: A woman in a grey suit smiles at the camera and holds her fist up in a soccer stadium

items.title: When moving forward means being the cycle-breaker in a family

children.content: Motherhood has a way of making family patterns impossible to ignore. Suddenly you start asking questions you never asked before.

gallery.altText: Carlie Schofield pictures as a baby, being held by her mum.

thumbnail.altText: Carlie Schofield pictures as a baby, being held by her mum.

avatar.alt: Carlie Schofield wears and maroon top smiling in a room with an Aboriginal artwork on the wall.

items.title: Reception hours slashed at police stations in Melbourne's outer-north

children.content: Victoria Police says its decision to cut the hours police stations are open to the public in Melbourne's outer north will result in more police on the frontline.

image.altText: A police badge on an arm.

items.title: NACC boss Paul Brereton admits he contributed to own 'downfall'

children.content: The corruption watchdog boss, appearing during a tense grilling at Senate estimates, admits his conduct became a "distraction".

image.altText: A grey-haired man with glasses, looking serious.

items.title: Grants allocated for soybean and rice trials in Mossman

children.content: The Queensland government will allocate $300,000 for new soybean and rice projects in Mossman as the region continues to search for a future beyond sugar cane.

image.altText: Farmer standing in soybean field in Mossman.

items.title: Hypersonic missile attack a sign of Putin's 'bruised ego', expert says

children.content: Russia escalates attacks on Kyiv by using a hypersonic missile in one of its largest assaults on the Ukrainian capital since the start of the four-year war, and warns foreigners to leave the city ahead of further strikes.

image.altText: A man with a bandaged head with blood on his t-shirt pats his dog while sheltering in an underground bomb shelter.

items.title: K-pop triumphs at American Music Awards as Pussycat Dolls lead noughties nostalgia

children.content: Taylor Swift led the night with eight nominations but didn't take home a single award, while 80s rock star Billy Idol was honoured with a Lifetime Achievement Award.

gallery.altText: Three women in red latex pose on stage.
Billy Idol in black leather and a purple shirt poses.

thumbnail.altText: Three women in red latex pose on stage.
Billy Idol in black leather and a purple shirt poses.

items.title: Romanian man jailed for stealing from elderly Australian shoppers

children.content: The Romanian citizen, who illegally visited Australia as part of a global "distraction fraud" operation, has been jailed after fleecing thousands of dollars from elderly Australian shoppers.

image.altText: Vasile Bombonel with his face blurred, holding two thumbs up while being escorted by police.

items.title: Police taskforce investigating Dezi Freeman arrests two men

children.content: Detectives investigating the movements of police killer Dezi Freeman have arrested two men in Victoria's north-east.

image.altText: Screengrab from footage of Dezi Freeman wearing sunglasses and gesturing while speaking with police.

items.title: After losing her 3yo daughter, one mum says MDMA therapy saved her life

children.content: Jess Yugovich is among the Australians trying MDMA-assisted psychotherapy after being diagnosed with PTSD, as highly regulated facilities process the drug for therapeutic use.

image.altText: Woman with short brown hair looks at camera in a green silk shirt and in a well lit therapy room.

items.title: Kumanjayi White family 'heartbroken' no charges laid over custody death

children.content: The family of Kumanjayi White tell the ABC they are "heartbroken" that no charges will be laid over his death in custody, as Northern Territory Police confirm their criminal investigation has been concluded.

image.altText: A group of Aboriginal mourners walking through a coles supermarket, while holding branches.

items.title: Man discovers Stolen Generations truth after decades believing he was adopted

children.content: Ricky Hampson spent most of his life believing he had been adopted before learning several years ago he is a survivor of the Stolen Generations. Today, he is calling for more action and truth-telling.

gallery.altText: A man looks serious in a shirt with the slogan "Always Was".

thumbnail.altText: A man looks serious in a shirt with the slogan "Always Was".
